reasoning: The starting value for 2026-03-15 (falling on a Sunday) corresponds to the March 13 close of 2.36%. This is near the high end of the recent 2.25%-2.38% range observed over the previous months. The statistical trend analysis gives a baseline 31% probability of the series exceeding 2.36% by March 22 (using the expected March 20 close). Additionally, the upcoming March 17-18 FOMC meeting is widely anticipated to reflect 'higher for longer' rates due to sticky inflation and a resilient labor market, with the Fed poised to adjust the dot plot hawkishly. Hawkish Fed meetings in response to inflation typically exert downward pressure on 10-year breakevens by driving up real yields (TIPS) as the market prices in the Fed's commitment to fighting inflation. Therefore, considering the high starting point, mean reversion tendencies, and the likely hawkish FOMC catalyst, an increase over 2.36% is relatively unlikely.
